import * as React from 'react'
  /* @jsx mdx */
import { mdx } from '@mdx-js/react';
/* @jsxRuntime classic */
/* @jsx mdx */
import DefaultLayout from "/home/runner/work/uxp-premiere-pro/uxp-premiere-pro/node_modules/@adobe/gatsby-theme-aio/src/components/MDXFilter/index.js";
export const _frontmatter = {};
const makeShortcode = name => function MDXDefaultShortcode(props) {
  console.warn("Component " + name + " was not imported, exported, or provided by MDXProvider as global scope");
  return <div {...props} />;
};
const InlineAlert = makeShortcode("InlineAlert");
const layoutProps = {
  _frontmatter
};
const MDXLayout = DefaultLayout;
export default function MDXContent({
  components,
  ...props
}) {
  return <MDXLayout {...layoutProps} {...props} components={components} mdxType="MDXLayout">


    <h1 {...{
      "id": "uxp-entrypoints"
    }}>{`UXP Entrypoints`}</h1>
    <p>{`Learn how to declare and implement plugin entrypoints for commands and panels in Premiere`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "plugin-components"
    }}>{`Plugin Components`}</h2>
    <p>{`UXP plugins are built using `}<strong parentName="p">{`Commands`}</strong>{` (menu items that execute code) and `}<strong parentName="p">{`Panels`}</strong>{` (persistent, dockable interfaces). A plugin can include one or both component types—and may define multiple instances of each.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`For a detailed explanation of the differences between Commands and Panels and when to use each, see the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../panels-and-commands/index.md"
      }}>{`Panels and Commands concept guide`}</a>{`.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "entrypoints"
    }}>{`Entrypoints`}</h2>
    <p>{`In UXP, an entrypoint is a specific way for the host application to invoke one of the plugin's components—commands or panels. You can think of it as a contract made of two different parts:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`A `}<strong parentName="li">{`Manifest file`}</strong>{` that declares the number and type of entrypoints the plugin provides.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Your `}<strong parentName="li">{`JavaScript code`}</strong>{` which handles the calls via `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`entrypoints.setup()`}</inlineCode>{` and maps the entrypoint ID to the corresponding implementation code.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "manifest-declaration"
    }}>{`Manifest declaration`}</h3>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{` file is located at the root of the plugin bundle that contains all sorts of metadata about the plugin. Among all its properties, documented in `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../manifest/index.md"
      }}>{`this reference guide`}</a>{`, Entrypoints are—unsurprisingly—declared in the required `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`entrypoints`}</inlineCode>{` array.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-json"
      }}>{`{
  "manifestVersion": 5,
  "id": "test1234",
  "name": "Test Plugin",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "host": { "app": "premierepro", "minVersion": "25.6.0" },
  "main": "index.html",
  "entrypoints": [                                  // 👈
      {                                             // 👈
          "type": "command",                        // 👈
          "id": "firstCommand",                     // 👈
          "label": { "default": "Run a Function" }  // 👈
      },                                            // 👈
      {                                             // 👈
          "type": "panel",                          // 👈
          "id": "firstPanel",                       // 👈
          "label": { "default": "First Panel" }     // 👈
      }                                             // 👈
  ],                                                // 👈
}
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`At least one entrypoint of type `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../manifest/index.md#entrypointdefinition"
      }}><inlineCode parentName="a">{`entrypointDefinition`}</inlineCode></a>{` must be declared in the Manifest.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "code-implementation"
    }}>{`Code implementation`}</h3>
    <p>{`Your JavaScript code (for example, in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`index.js`}</inlineCode>{` file) handles the calls each time the host application invokes one of the Entrypoints. From the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`uxp`}</inlineCode>{` module, you can access the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`entrypoints`}</inlineCode>{` object, whose `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`setup()`}</inlineCode>{` method is what we'll focus on in the remainder of this guide.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`const { entrypoints } = require("uxp");
entrypoints.setup({ /* ... */ });
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "the-entrypointssetup-method"
    }}>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="h2">{`entrypoints.setup()`}</inlineCode>{` method`}</h2>
    <p>{`Although the implementation of the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`entrypoints.setup()`}</inlineCode>{` method is optional, you are strongly encouraged to provide it. It will:`}</p>
    <ol>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Define `}<strong parentName="li">{`lifecycle hooks at a Plugin`}</strong>{` level.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Define `}<strong parentName="li">{`lifecycle hooks at a Panel`}</strong>{` level.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Allow the creation of `}<strong parentName="li">{`flyout menus`}</strong>{` and hook up the respective handlers for each Panel entrypoint.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ol">{`Define `}<strong parentName="li">{`handlers for each Command`}</strong>{` entrypoint.`}</li>
    </ol>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="header, text, text2" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`Lifecycle hooks`}</p>
    <p>{`These are callback functions that are automatically invoked when specific events occur during the lifecycle of a Plugin or a Panel; they are optional and can be used to perform, for example, any setup or teardown logic.`}</p>
    <p>{`Since Manifest version 5, most lifecycle `}<strong parentName="p">{`handlers may be asynchronous`}</strong>{` and return a `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Promise"
      }}>{`Promise`}</a>{`—see `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "#using-promises"
      }}>{`Using Promises`}</a>{` below for an example.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "plugin-lifecycle-hooks"
    }}>{`Plugin Lifecycle Hooks`}</h3>
    <p>{`UXP provides two lifecycle hooks at a Plugin level, respectively invoked when the plugin container itself is created and destroyed: `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`create()`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`destroy()`}</inlineCode>{`. You define them in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`plugin`}</inlineCode>{` property of the object passed to the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`entrypoints.setup()`}</inlineCode>{` method.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`const { entrypoints } = require("uxp");
entrypoints.setup({
  plugin: {
    create()  { console.log("Plugin created");   },
    destroy() { console.log("Plugin destroyed"); }
  },
});
`}</code></pre>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "panel-lifecycle-hooks"
    }}>{`Panel Lifecycle Hooks`}</h3>
    <p>{`At a panel level, you have access to more lifecycle hooks: `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`create()`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`show()`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`hide()`}</inlineCode>{`, and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`destroy()`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`You define them in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`panels`}</inlineCode>{` property of the object passed to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`entrypoints.setup()`}</inlineCode>{`.
This object must include one property for each `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"panel"`}</inlineCode>{` entrypoint declared in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{`, using the panel IDs as property names.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`const { entrypoints } = require("uxp");
entrypoints.setup({
  panels: {
    firstPanel: {  // 👈 must match the id of the entrypoint
                   //    of type "panel" from manifest.json
      create(rootNode)  { console.log("Panel created"); },
      show(rootNode)    { console.log("Panel shown"); },
      hide(rootNode)    { console.log("Panel hidden"); },
      destroy(rootNode) { console.log("Panel destroyed"); }
    },
    secondPanel: { /*...*/ }, // 👈  add properties for each additional
                              //    "panel" entrypoint in manifest.json
  }
});
`}</code></pre>
    <InlineAlert variant="warning" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`hide()`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`destroy()`}</inlineCode>{` hooks are `}<strong parentName="p">{`not working as expected yet`}</strong>{` in Premiere. This will be fixed in a future update.`}</p>
    <p>{`Panel handlers receive a `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`rootNode`}</inlineCode>{` parameter; it's the HTML document root node, which can be used to programmatically append or remove an appropriate container element in a multiple panels scenario.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "command-handlers"
    }}>{`Command Handlers`}</h3>
    <p>{`Command handlers are functions that are invoked when a command entrypoint is invoked via click to Premiere `}<strong parentName="p">{`Window`}</strong>{` > `}<strong parentName="p">{`UXP Plugins`}</strong>{` > `}<strong parentName="p"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`<your plugin name>`}</inlineCode></strong>{` > `}<strong parentName="p"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`<your command name>`}</inlineCode></strong>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`They are defined in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`commands`}</inlineCode>{` property of the object passed to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`entrypoints.setup()`}</inlineCode>{`, which must include one property for each `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`"command"`}</inlineCode>{` entrypoint declared in the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`manifest.json`}</inlineCode>{`, using the command IDs as property names.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`const { entrypoints } = require("uxp");

const commandHandler = (evt) => {
  console.log(
    "Command handler invoked!", evt.type // uxpcommand
  );
};

entrypoints.setup({
  commands: {
    firstCommand: commandHandler, // 👈 must match the id of the
                                  // entrypoint of type "command"
                                  // from manifest.json
  }
});
`}</code></pre>
    <p>{`The handler function receives a single event parameter of type `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`uxpcommand`}</inlineCode>{`. It can be listened to at the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`<body>`}</inlineCode>{` element level, if needed.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`document.body.addEventListener("uxpcommand" (event) => { /* ... */ });
`}</code></pre>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "using-promises"
    }}>{`Using Promises`}</h2>
    <p>{`Promise support in UXP entrypoints is enabled for:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Plugins`}</strong>{`: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`destroy()`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Panels`}</strong>{`: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`create()`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`show()`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`destroy()`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`hide()`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`Currently, the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`show()`}</inlineCode>{` hook is tied to the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`create()`}</inlineCode>{` hook, and the `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`hide()`}</inlineCode>{` hook is tied to `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`destroy()`}</inlineCode>{`. All methods have a timeout of 300 milliseconds.`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-javascript"
      }}>{`const { entrypoints } = require("uxp");
entrypoints.setup({
  plugin: {
    create() {
      console.log("Plugin create hook");
    },
    destroy() {
      return new Promise(function (resolve, reject) {
        console.log("Plugin destroy hook");
        resolve();
      });
    },
  },
  panels: {
    firstPanel: {
      create(rootNode) {
        return new Promise(function (resolve, reject) {
          console.log("Panel create hook", rootNode);
          resolve();
        });
      },
      show(rootNode, data) {
        return new Promise(function (resolve, reject) {
          console.log("Panel show hook", data);
          resolve();
        });
      },
      hide(rootNode, data) {
        return new Promise(function (resolve, reject) {
          console.log("Panel hide hook", data);
          resolve();
        });
      },
      destroy(rootNode) {
        return new Promise(function (resolve, reject) {
          console.log("Panel destroy hook", rootNode);
          resolve();
        });
      },
    },
  },
  commands: {
    firstCommand: commandHandler,
  },
});
`}</code></pre>

    </MDXLayout>;
}
;
MDXContent.isMDXComponent = true;
